introductions.default: Principal-level architect and engineering lead with six years designing and shipping research-grade AI and security systems at national-security scale.
introductions.architecture: Principal-level architect and engineering lead. I work at the architecture layer — platform patterns, integration contracts, evaluation frameworks — and prove the design by building it. Currently architect across NetSTAR's full security-solutions portfolio, shaping platforms that downstream engineering teams build against.
introductions.ml-ai: Senior ML / AI engineer with six years shipping research-grade systems into production. LLM categorization pipelines classifying 40M+ domains quarterly, Graph Neural Network architectures, MCP servers, and AI model-security scanners on Temporal durable workflows.
introductions.cybersecurity: Cybersecurity researcher and threat-intelligence architect. Designed platforms unifying STIX/TAXII, MITRE ATT&CK, and federal CISA AIS feeds; initiated NetSTAR Shield end-user protection; peer-reviewed research on AI-driven threat detection.
introductions.research: Applied researcher at the intersection of AI and cybersecurity. Published work on semantic technologies for cybersecurity education and AI-driven threat detection (University of Arizona). Pursuing M.S. in Cyber & Information Operations.
introductions.leadership: Technical leader driving architectural direction across AI and security programs. Own cross-team integration contracts, platform patterns, and research-to-production delivery cadence.
